I was untouched.”  
- “There was noise, but no one *heard* me.”  
- “I didn't want sex. I wanted to disappear into someone else's warmth.”  
- “Touch became my god, and I worshiped it in private.”

These are not the confessions of broken people. They are the whispered histories of those who *had to build their own sacredness* when no one taught them how to receive it.

---

### ✦ On Addiction, Ritual, and Meaning

Many describe the behavior becoming **ritualistic**, even **spiritual**, before it was pathologized. Something about it *felt holy and secret*—until shame or culture pressed labels on it.
